key_drivers: CME FedWatch for the 30 Apr 2026 meeting shows 0.0% ease, 99.5% no change, and 0.5% hike, which keeps the Fed input in a hawkish-hold regime and leaves the dollar modestly firm.

The official March central-bank backdrop is diverging: the RBA raised the cash rate to 4.10%, the BoJ said it will continue raising rates if the outlook is realized, while the ECB held at 2.00% and eurozone growth projections were revised down.
Crude has the cleanest bullish setup because the latest official EIA data showed a commercial crude draw and the broader Gulf shipping backdrop still implies tightening oil-supply risk.
Equity leadership is selective rather than broad: SOX rose 2.43% and MOVE fell 0.30%, but HY OAS ticked up to 2.86 and DXY was higher on the day, which caps broad-index conviction.

Precious-metals support from ongoing World Gold Council ETF inflows is partly offset by a firmer dollar, flat real yields, and a falling copper signal that weakens the silver and AUD industrial-demand backdrop.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remains the 9 Apr 2026 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 latest official observations lag the report timestamp by standard publication delay.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence is derived from the 13 Apr 2026 Weekly Markets Monitor rather than a same-day holdings table.
fallbacks_used: The visible CME FedWatch probability table in the saved screenshot was used directly because the page text did not expose the full meeting row cleanly.
The official World Gold Council Weekly Markets Monitor PDF was used to confirm continued ETF inflows.
The official ECB bulletin, RBA March 17 release, BoJ March 19 statement, and official China PMI release were used for cross-central-bank and China-growth inputs.
overnight_changes: DXY was up 0.12% on the day, reinforcing a modest dollar headwind for commodities and non-USD FX.
VIX remained balanced at 17.48 and effectively flat, while MOVE was lower and SOX was higher, favoring selective growth leadership rather than broad risk-on.
Real yields were effectively flat at 1.93 versus 1.90 the prior reading, HY OAS widened slightly to 2.86 from 2.85, and the 2s10s curve steepened to 0.55 from 0.54.

imminent: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update scheduled for April 21, 2026
near_term: Any further Strait of Hormuz or Gulf shipping escalation could quickly reprice crude and safe havens
background: RBA hawkish March hike continues to support AUD rate differentials
BoJ normalization rhetoric remains supportive for yen strength on a multi-session basis
